items.q: What gas does yeast produce that makes bread rise?
items.why: Students confuse yeast with photosynthesis. Yeast ferments sugar to ETHANOL and CO2 (anaerobic respiration). The CO2 gets trapped by gluten, expanding the dough. Ethanol bakes off.

items.q: Which macronutrient provides the most energy per gram?
items.why: Students assume carbs (the 'energy food') provide most energy per gram. Carbs and protein = 4 kcal/g; FAT = 9 kcal/g (most energy-dense). Vitamins provide no energy at all.

items.correct: Acidic conditions (low pH) prevent most bacteria from growing.
items.misconception: The vinegar kills the food cells.
items.why: Students think pickling poisons bacteria. It works by creating an environment INHOSPITABLE to most microbes - low pH stops them growing, even if they're present.

items.q: Which test asks tasters to identify the odd sample?
items.why: It's Triangle. “Hedonic” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Degree of liking. “Order of sweetness” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many samples in one triangle test?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
